description: Integer prime factorization modelled on the primefac library: isprime/iterprod/primefac with a Fundamental-Theorem-of-Arithmetic closure on the factorization anchored on the frozen divisibility relation. The load-bearing anchor is spec_isprime_oracle, a biconditional pinning the primality test to the frozen `∣` (isprime n ↔ 2 ≤ n ∧ every divisor is 1 or n) — a unique mathematical characterization, NOT agreement with a copyable sieve body — so both degenerate constant predicates are rejected and every downstream factorization law inherits genuine primality. The factorization is pinned by the FTA closure (product = n via the frozen iterprod product, every element prime via the pinned isprime, output sorted nondecreasing via the frozen ≤), upgraded to a unique-answer characterization by spec_primefac_unique (any sorted prime list multiplying to n equals primefac n), so no lookup-table impl over the unbounded Nat domain can pass. The isprime completeness obligations are stated in BOTH directions (no nontrivial divisor of a prime, and primality from the absence of small divisors) and the composite-witness existential is paired with its completeness partner; the deep factorization obligations include uniqueness, prime-self (a prime factors to itself), prime-divisor membership (no prime factor is omitted), and per-element divisibility. iterprod freezes the product operation (nil/cons/append/ground-truth-agreement) so the product law cannot be satisfied by a fudged product. Scope: the benchmark models the closed package core (isprime, the list product iterprod, primefac) over Nat; it does NOT model the probabilistic primality tests, Pollard-rho/ECM factoring engines, or the multiplicity (factorint exponent-dictionary) views of the upstream library — the factorization is returned as a nondecreasing Nat list, the canonical presentation of the prime multiset.
